# ProductSpec: Sherpaa
*Generated by PM Agent | April 11, 2026*

---

## Problem & Opportunity

Cyclists who use Strava to hunt segments have no in-ear coaching during their rides. Strava's audio cues exist for running but have never been extended to cycling — a gap the community has actively requested since 2014 with no delivery in sight. Head unit alerts (Garmin, Wahoo) give rigid, metric-only prompts with zero personalization. AI training platforms (CoachCat, Spoked, Athletica) deliver feedback hours after the ride ends, too late to change behavior. The result: a rider approaching their PR segment has no voice in their ear telling them what they did wrong last time, what wattage to hold, or whether they're currently ahead of pace.

The opportunity is a first-mover product that sits at the intersection of three existing, mature infrastructure layers — Strava's segment database, iOS on-device GPS, and on-device text-to-speech — and wires them together with a pre-generated personalized coaching layer. No single competitor has combined all three. The window is open specifically because Strava has structural reluctance to build voice features for cycling, and because bone conduction headphones (Shokz, etc.) have solved the outdoor audio safety problem at scale.

**Market signal:** Strava's community thread requesting cycling audio cues has been open for 12+ years. The mountain bike segment alone reports average household income of €70,900 and next-bike budgets of ~€5,300, indicating premium willingness-to-pay. At ~$0.006/ride COGS and $6.99/month Pro pricing, gross margin at scale exceeds 95%.

---

## Positioning

**One-line:** Sherpaa is the AI segment coach that rides in your ear — delivering pre-generated, personally tailored voice guidance on every Strava segment before you could even glance at your head unit.

**Category:** Real-time AI cycling coach (new category; closest adjacent is Nike Run Club Guided Runs, which has no cycling equivalent)

**Against Strava Live Segments:** Strava shows you a number on a screen. Sherpaa speaks to you, knows your history on this specific segment, and tells you what to do differently this time.

**Against Garmin/Wahoo alerts:** They alert. Sherpaa coaches. Alert = "You are 3 seconds behind pace." Coach = "You always go out too hot here — hold 280 watts through the false flat, save your kick for the top."

**Against AI training platforms:** They analyze yesterday. Sherpaa coaches right now.

---

## Target Users & Personas

### Persona 1 — The Segment Hunter ("Carlos")
- **Age:** 38 | **Location:** Bay Area, CA | **Income:** $140K HHI
- **Setup:** Garmin Edge 540 + Shokz OpenRun Pro, Strava Summit subscriber, 12 starred segments on his regular routes
- **Rides:** 4x/week, ~200 miles/month, mostly road
- **Behavior:** Checks Strava leaderboard before every ride. Knows his PRs on Hawk Hill and Paradise Loop to the second. Frequently frustrated that his Garmin shows him the gap only after it's too late to respond.
- **Quote:** *"I know I always blow up on the false flat. I just can't feel it happening until I'm already cooked."*
- **Job to be done:** Get segment-specific, real-time feedback that helps him execute strategy, not just observe metrics.
- **Willingness to pay:** $6.99–$12.99/month without hesitation if the coaching is accurate.

### Persona 2 — The Returning Cyclist ("Diana")
- **Age:** 44 | **Location:** Marin County, CA | **Income:** $210K HHI
- **Setup:** Canyon Endurace, AirPods Pro (uses transparency mode), Strava free tier
- **Rides:** 2–3x/week, returning to fitness after a 2-year gap, no power meter
- **Behavior:** Has 6 starred segments but hasn't PRed any of them in over a year. Uses recent-best as her reference point, not all-time PR. Doesn't care about leaderboards — competes only against herself.
- **Quote:** *"I don't need someone to tell me my average speed. I need someone to tell me if I'm having a good day or not, and what to do about it."*
- **Job to be done:** Understand how today's effort compares to her recent baseline, with encouragement that's calibrated to where she actually is, not where she was in 2021.
- **Willingness to pay:** $6.99/month (Pro); resistant to Elite unless fatigue detection proves useful.

### Persona 3 — The Competitive Amateur ("Marcus")
- **Age:** 32 | **Location:** San Francisco, CA | **Income:** $95K HHI
- **Setup:** Wahoo ELEMNT Bolt, Garmin HRM-Pro, power meter (Favero Assioma), Strava Summit
- **Rides:** 6x/week, races crits and gran fondos, structured training with a paid plan from TrainingPeaks
- **Behavior:** Every ride is in Training Peaks. Uses Strava for segments socially. Wants power-zone coaching during segments, not just time-based.
- **Quote:** *"ACE from Wahoo doesn't have Strava Live Segments. Strava doesn't talk to me. There's a gap right in the middle and no one's filled it."*
- **Job to be done:** Power-zone pacing guidance during segments that integrates with his existing structured training, plus trend analysis across rides.
- **Willingness to pay:** $12.99/month (Elite) — power coaching and trend analysis are the unlock.

### Persona 4 — The Club Rider ("Priya")
- **Age:** 29 | **Location:** Berkeley, CA | **Income:** $80K HHI
- **Setup:** Trek Domane, Bose Sport earbuds, Strava free tier
- **Rides:** Weekend group rides with MCBC, occasional solo training rides
- **Behavior:** Joined a cycling club 18 months ago. Has starred 10 segments based on club recommendations. Mostly competes against the club KOM board. Doesn't have a training plan.
- **Quote:** *"My club friends kept telling me to try for the Hawk Hill KOM. But I have no idea how to pace it. I just go hard and hope."*
- **Job to be done:** A knowledgeable voice that provides race-day-style tactical guidance even without a coach.
- **Willingness to pay:** Will start on Free tier. Converts to Pro if she sees improvement in 30 days.

### Persona 5 — The Data-Driven Masters Rider ("Tom")
- **Age:** 51 | **Location:** Mill Valley, CA | **Income:** $290K HHI
- **Setup:** Full Shimano Di2 groupset, Quarq power meter, Hammerhead Karoo 3, Strava Summit, Whoop 4.0
- **Rides:** 5x/week. Has been riding for 20 years. Deeply knowledgeable about his physiology.
- **Behavior:** Tracks HRV, sleep, and load every day. Adjusts intensity based on readiness. Wants his coaching app to know when he's fatigued before he does.
- **Quote:** *"I've set every PR on Hawk Hill before 8am. After 3pm I'm always 15 seconds slower. I want an app that knows that."*
- **Job to be done:** Adaptive guidance that incorporates fatigue signals and time-of-day patterns into real-time pacing decisions.
- **Willingness to pay:** $12.99/month (Elite); would pay $25/month if trend analysis were richer.

---

## Competitive Landscape

| Product | Real-time? | Voice? | Segment-aware? | Personalized? | Cycling? | Price |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Sherpaa** | Yes | Yes | Yes (Strava starred) | Yes (per-segment history) | Yes | $6.99–$12.99/mo |
| Strava Live Segments | Yes | No | Yes | No | Yes | Bundled w/ Summit $7.99/mo |
| Garmin Connect (head unit alerts) | Yes | Partial (alert tone + stat) | Limited | No | Yes | $0 (device required) |
| Wahoo ELEMNT ACE | Yes | Alert-only | No (no Strava Live at launch) | No | Yes | $0 (device required) |
| Nike Run Club Guided Runs | Yes (run only) | Yes | No | Partial | No | $14.99/mo (NRC+) |
| CoachCat | No (post-ride) | No | No | Yes | Yes | $35/mo |
| Spoked | No (post-ride) | No | No | Yes | Yes | $19/mo |
| Athletica | No (post-ride) | No | No | Yes | Yes | $15/mo |
| MapMyRide | Partial | Stat readout only | No | No | Yes | $5.99/mo |
| Cyclemeter | Partial | Stat readout only | No | No | Yes | $4.99/mo |

**Sherpaa is the only product that combines all four differentiating attributes simultaneously.**

---

## Feature List (MoSCoW)

### Must Have (v1)

**Strava Integration**
- M1: OAuth 2.0 Strava authentication with `activity:read_all` and `read` scopes
- M2: Fetch user's starred segments at ride start (cached; refreshed daily or on manual pull)
- M3: Fetch last 90 days of segment efforts per starred segment at ride start
- M4: Post-ride activity sync to Strava

**Pre-Ride Flow**
- M5: Route selection screen with segment overlay (identifies starred segments on intended route)
- M6: Spoken pre-ride brief: segment count, top 3 PR targets, personal tendency summary
- M7: Goal mode selection: PR Attempt, Training Effort, or Recovery Ride
- M8: Pre-generation of 3 cue variants (Aggressive/Moderate/Recovery) per on-route segment at ride start, over WiFi/cellular

**In-Ride Coaching**
- M9: 1Hz on-device GPS polling with geofence detection against cached segment coordinates
- M10: 500m approach cue: segment name, PR time, personal tendency, pacing instruction
- M11: Segment start confirmation cue
- M12: Mid-segment rule-based cues tied to pace/HR vs. PR split (template-based, no LLM call)
- M13: Segment end result cue (time, gap to PR, new PR flag)
- M14: Variant selection at geofence trigger based on live HR/power reading (no network call)
- M15: Between-segment silence by default; elevated-HR check-in cue if threshold exceeded

**Post-Ride**
- M16: Spoken post-ride debrief (segment results, best effort, trend note)
- M17: Strava activity sync post-ride
- M18: Pacing model update after each completed segment

**Core App**
- M19: Offline-first architecture: all cues pre-cached; zero network calls during ride
- M20: On-device TTS via iOS AVSpeechSynthesizer (no audio file caching)
- M21: Background app operation during ride (iOS background modes: location, audio)
- M22: Free tier: 3 starred segments, pace-only generic cues
- M23: Pro tier unlock: unlimited segments, full PR Coach, splits analysis, HR coaching, post-ride debrief

### Should Have (v1.1)

- S1: "Recent Best" and "Last Best" as alternative PR targets (not just all-time)
- S2: Time-of-day pattern detection ("You're 15s faster on this segment before 10am")
- S3: Apple Health integration for resting HR and HRV readiness input
- S4: Segment cue history (review what was said after the ride)
- S5: Notification when cue cache is stale and needs regeneration

### Could Have (v2)

- C1: Real-time LLM fallback cue on significant mid-ride deviation (HR spike, stop, crash detection)
- C2: Multi-route pre-generation (generate cues for 2–3 saved routes in advance)
- C3: Club segment leaderboard awareness ("You're 3rd in your club on this segment")
- C4: Wahoo/Garmin Bluetooth companion display data feed

### Won't Have (v1)

- W1: MTB trail mode
- W2: Coach Mode (third-party coach access)
- W3: Android support
- W4: Group ride / social features
- W5: On-device ML model (Core ML) — v3+ consideration
- W6: Real-time LLM during ride
- W7: Custom segment creation (non-Strava)

---

## User Flows

---

### Flow 1 — Onboarding + Strava Connect

**Actor:** New user, first app launch
**Trigger:** User downloads Sherpaa from App Store and opens for the first time
**Preconditions:** User has a Strava account with at least 1 starred segment; iOS 16+; internet connection

**Steps:**
1. App displays welcome screen: product name, one-line value prop ("Your AI segment coach. In your ear. Right now."), CTA "Get Started"
2. App presents 3-screen feature carousel: (a) Segment-aware coaching, (b) Pre-generated offline cues, (c) Spoken debrief
3. App requests Strava OAuth: opens Strava authorization URL in ASWebAuthenticationSession. Requested scopes: `read`, `activity:read_all`
4. User authenticates on Strava's page, grants permissions
5. App receives OAuth token + refresh token; stores securely in iOS Keychain
6. App fetches athlete profile (name, profile photo) to confirm connection
7. App requests iOS Location permission (Always or While Using — explain why: segment detection during ride)
8. App requests iOS Microphone permission (not required for TTS output — skip if unneeded; flag for review)
9. App displays "Connected as [Name]" confirmation with Strava avatar
10. App initiates background fetch of starred segments (non-blocking)
11. App navigates to Home screen

**Success outcome:** User's Strava account is linked; starred segments are fetched; user lands on Home screen ready to plan a ride

**Failure paths:**
- Strava OAuth denied: display error screen, "Sherpaa needs Strava access to find your segments. Tap to try again." Retry button re-initiates OAuth
- Strava OAuth token exchange fails (network error): show retry screen with offline notice
- Zero starred segments found: display empty state "You have no starred segments yet. Star segments in Strava to unlock coaching." Deep link to Strava app
- Location permission denied: display warning banner "Segment detection requires location access. Open Settings to enable."

**UX notes:**
- OAuth must use `ASWebAuthenticationSession` (not deprecated `SFSafariViewController`) per iOS best practices
- Keychain storage only — never UserDefaults for tokens
- Do not request "Always" location on first prompt if avoidable; request "While Using" and upgrade to "Always" at ride-start time with contextual explanation

---

### Flow 2 — Route Setup & Pre-Ride Brief

**Actor:** Authenticated user preparing for a ride
**Trigger:** User taps "Plan Ride" on Home screen
**Preconditions:** Strava connected; at least 1 starred segment in cache; internet connection (WiFi preferred for cue generation)

**Steps:**
1. App displays Route Setup screen: split layout — top half is a dark map, bottom half is a scrollable list of the rider's 10 most recent Strava activities that include a GPS track
2. User selects route method: (a) pick a recent Strava ride — tap to preview its polyline on the map, tap "Use this route" to confirm, (b) Import GPX (v1.1 — stub button shows "Coming soon"), (c) Skip (app will detect segments dynamically during ride)
3. On preview, app decodes the activity's summary polyline, renders it on the map, and identifies which starred segments intersect the route (start + end within 150m of any polyline point); on confirmation, segment list replaces the ride list with estimated segment order and distance along route
4. App displays Goal Mode selector: PR Attempt | Training Effort | Recovery Ride. Default = Training Effort
5. User confirms or changes goal mode
6. App displays cue generation status: "Generating coaching cues for [N] segments… (WiFi recommended)"
7. Background: for each on-route segment, LLM call generates 3 cue variants (Aggressive, Moderate, Recovery). Cues stored as text strings in local DB. Input = rider's last 90 days effort history on segment + personal tendency flags.
8. On completion: app plays spoken pre-ride brief. Example: *"You have 4 segments today. Hawk Hill PR is 4:12 — you've faded in the final 400m the last three attempts. Lagunas PR is 7:44. Goal mode: Training Effort. Ready when you are."*
9. App displays "Start Ride" CTA and "Edit Goals" option
10. User taps "Start Ride" → transitions to In-Ride screen

**Success outcome:** Cues generated for all on-route segments; spoken pre-brief delivered; user in ride-ready state

**Failure paths:**
- No internet at cue generation step: use cached cues if available (< 30 days old); display banner "Using cached coaching cues — connect to WiFi before your next ride to refresh"
- LLM generation failure for one segment: fall back to generic template cues for that segment; do not block ride start
- Route contains 0 starred segments: display "No starred segments on this route. Coaching will activate if you ride near any of your starred segments." Allow ride start anyway
- Strava segment fetch fails: use local cache; display timestamp of last sync

**UX notes:**
- Cue generation should happen as soon as route is confirmed, not at ride-start moment
- Progress indicator should be non-blocking — user can continue planning while generation runs
- If on cellular (not WiFi), display a data usage warning (~50–200KB per segment for LLM API calls)
- Spoken pre-brief uses the same AVSpeechSynthesizer voice as in-ride cues for consistency

---

### Flow 3 — In-Ride Experience

**Actor:** Rider on route
**Trigger:** User taps "Start Ride"; GPS lock confirmed
**Preconditions:** Cues pre-generated and cached; GPS active; earbuds connected

**Steps:**

**[Ride Start]**
1. App confirms GPS lock (display satellite icon, coordinate accuracy)
2. Spoken start cue: *"GPS locked. [N] segments loaded. Goal: Training Effort. Let's go."*
3. App enters background mode: location updates at 1Hz, audio session active

**[En Route — Between Segments]**
4. App polls GPS at 1Hz; calculates distance to next starred segment start coordinate using Haversine formula
5. If rider HR > threshold (configurable, default 85% max HR) for > 5 minutes between segments: plays check-in cue. Example: *"18 minutes to Lagunas. Heart rate elevated — ease up, save your legs."*
6. Otherwise: silence

**[Approaching Segment — 500m out]**
7. Geofence trigger fires at 500m from segment start
8. App reads live HR/power; selects cue variant: Aggressive (HR below 75% max), Moderate (75–85%), Recovery (above 85% or goal mode = Recovery)
9. Plays 500m approach cue. Example: *"Hawk Hill in 500 meters. PR is 4:12. You tend to fade in the final 400m — hold 280 watts through the false flat, save your kick for the top."*

**[Segment Active]**
10. Segment start detected: plays brief start cue. Example: *"Hawk Hill. Go."*
11. App begins comparing GPS-inferred pace against cached PR split paces at 10% interval checkpoints
12. At pre-defined split checkpoints (configurable, default: 25%, 50%, 75% of segment distance): plays mid-segment cue if deviation from PR split exceeds threshold (±5 seconds). Example: *"Halfway. You're 3 seconds up on PR pace — hold."*
13. Segment end detected (GPS crosses segment end coordinate)
14. Calculates elapsed time; plays result cue. If new PR: *"4:08. New PR. 4 seconds."* If not: *"4:18. 6 seconds off PR. Strong finish."*
15. Records SegmentEffort to local DB; flags for Strava sync post-ride

**[Post-Segment]**
16. Returns to between-segment state (step 4)

**Success outcome:** Rider receives approach, start, split, and result cues for every segment; no network calls occur during ride; app runs uninterrupted in background

**Failure paths:**
- GPS signal lost: app marks gap in track; does not fire segment triggers without valid GPS fix; plays cue: *"GPS signal weak — coaching paused."* Resumes automatically when signal recovers
- Earbuds disconnect: TTS cues queue; resume delivery when audio session re-established; no silent data loss
- Segment coordinates mismatch (GPS drift): geofence has a 25m tolerance buffer around segment start/end; if segment not triggered within 200m of expected start, skip segment and log for review
- Rider stops (speed < 2 km/h for > 60s): app pauses segment detection; checks for crash pattern; plays: *"Looks like you've stopped. Ride paused."* Resumes on motion detection
- App backgrounded by OS: relies on iOS significant-location-change and background-location APIs; no cues lost because all audio is pre-buffered

**UX notes:**
- Screen stays dark during ride (respect battery); minimal foreground UI
- Foreground screen when active: current speed, HR (if connected), distance to next segment, next segment name
- Volume management: Sherpaa audio session uses `.playback` category with `.mixWithOthers` option disabled — pauses music/podcast, speaks, resumes (standard iOS behavior)
- Variant selection happens at 500m geofence, not at ride start — to use the most current HR/power reading

---

### Flow 4 — Post-Ride Debrief

**Actor:** Rider who has completed a ride
**Trigger:** User taps "End Ride" button, or ride auto-ends after 30 minutes of inactivity
**Preconditions:** At least 1 segment was completed during the ride; Strava OAuth token valid

**Steps:**
1. App stops GPS polling and location updates
2. Plays spoken debrief. Example: *"Ride complete. You hit 3 of 4 segments. New PR on Hawk Hill: 4:08. Lagunas was 8 seconds off PR — you were strong early and faded. Paradise Loop: skipped, looks like you took the alternate route. Overall a solid effort. Data syncing to Strava."*
3. App displays post-ride summary screen: map of ride, segment cards (PR/no PR, time, gap), overall stats
4. Background: pushes activity data to Strava via API (POST /api/v3/uploads or activity creation)
5. Background: updates PacingModel per segment — records actual pace at each 25% checkpoint vs. PR checkpoint; flags tendency patterns (fade, strong-start, consistent)
6. Background: marks SegmentEfforts as synced in local DB
7. App flags any segments where cues are now stale (new PR set → regenerate next time)
8. App displays "Share" option (native iOS share sheet — share ride summary image, not raw Strava data)
9. User taps "Done" → returns to Home screen

**Success outcome:** Spoken debrief delivered; Strava sync complete; PacingModel updated; stale cue flags set

**Failure paths:**
- Strava sync fails (network error): queue sync for retry with exponential backoff; display banner "Strava sync pending — will retry when connected"; local data preserved
- Debrief generation fails: fall back to text-only summary screen; no spoken debrief
- PacingModel update fails: log error, retry next ride start; do not block post-ride flow

**UX notes:**
- Spoken debrief should complete before screen appears (estimated 25–40 seconds audio)
- Post-ride summary screen is the only screen with heavy visual data — this is the "reward" moment; design for pride and sharing
- Do not auto-navigate away from summary screen

---

### Flow 5 — Subscription Upgrade

**Actor:** Free-tier user who has hit the 3-segment limit or tried to access a Pro feature
**Trigger:** (a) User approaches a 4th starred segment during ride and sees "Upgrade to unlock coaching on this segment" prompt, OR (b) User taps a locked feature in Settings or Pre-Ride screen
**Preconditions:** User is on Free tier; Strava connected

**Steps:**
1. Paywall screen appears (full-screen modal, not a banner): headline "Unlock Your Full Segment Coach"
2. Displays feature comparison: Free vs. Pro vs. Elite (table format matching monetization tier definitions)
3. Displays pricing: Pro $6.99/month or $59/year (save 30%); Elite $12.99/month or $99/year (save 37%)
4. Default selection: Pro annual (best value highlighted)
5. User selects plan and taps "Start [X]-Day Free Trial" (7-day free trial for Pro; 7-day for Elite)
6. iOS StoreKit payment sheet appears (standard Apple IAP flow)
7. User confirms purchase with Face ID / Apple Pay
8. App receives StoreKit transaction confirmation; updates entitlement in local DB + backend
9. Paywall dismisses; user returned to prior context (pre-ride screen, in-ride prompt dismissed)
10. If triggered mid-ride (step 4a): app immediately activates Pro features for remaining ride without interruption

**Success outcome:** User subscribed; entitlement activated immediately; StoreKit receipt stored for restore

**Failure paths:**
- Payment fails: StoreKit surfaces native error; app shows "Payment was not completed — try again or use a different payment method"
- StoreKit sandbox issue (during TestFlight): documented in internal test guide; not visible to users
- User dismisses paywall: returns to prior context; Free tier continues; no nagging (do not re-show paywall for 48 hours after dismissal)
- Restore purchases: "Restore Purchases" link on paywall surfaces previous subscription

**UX notes:**
- Paywall must not appear mid-segment (never interrupt a segment in progress); queue until segment end result cue plays
- Annual plans should be default-selected to maximize LTV
- 7-day free trial must be disclosed clearly per Apple App Store Review Guidelines 3.1.2
- No dark patterns: cancellation must be clearly described (cancel anytime in Settings > Subscriptions)

---

## Screen Definitions

---

### Screen 1 — Welcome / Splash

**Purpose:** Brand first impression; route to onboarding or (if returning user) straight to Home
**Layout:** Full-bleed cycling imagery; logo centered; tagline below; single CTA
**Components:** App logo, tagline ("Your AI segment coach. In your ear. Right now."), "Get Started" button, "I already have an account" text link
**User actions:** Tap "Get Started" → Onboarding Carousel; Tap "I already have an account" → Strava OAuth (skip carousel)
**Empty state:** N/A (always shows imagery)
**Navigation:** Entry point; no back

---

### Screen 2 — Onboarding Carousel

**Purpose:** Explain the product's 3 core value props before asking for Strava access
**Layout:** Horizontal pager, 3 cards, progress dots, skip button top-right
**Components:** Illustration per card, headline (max 6 words), 1-sentence body, "Next" / "Connect Strava" on final card
- Card 1: "Your coach knows this climb." — Pre-generated, personalized cues for every starred segment
- Card 2: "In your ear. Not on your screen." — Voice coaching via bone conduction or any earbuds
- Card 3: "Gets smarter every ride." — Pacing model learns your tendencies over time
**User actions:** Swipe or tap "Next" to advance; tap "Skip" to jump to Strava OAuth; tap "Connect Strava" on card 3
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** Progresses to Strava OAuth

---

### Screen 3 — Strava OAuth (System Web View)

**Purpose:** OAuth authorization via `ASWebAuthenticationSession`
**Layout:** Strava's hosted authorization page; app has no control over layout
**Components:** Strava UI (email/password or existing session); scope disclosure ("Sherpaa wants access to your activities and starred segments")
**User actions:** Authorize or Deny
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** Success → Location Permission prompt; Deny → Error screen (Screen 4)

---

### Screen 4 — OAuth Error / Retry

**Purpose:** Handle Strava connect failure gracefully
**Layout:** Centered error state
**Components:** Error icon, headline "Couldn't connect to Strava", body copy (reason), "Try Again" primary button, "Contact Support" text link
**User actions:** Tap "Try Again" → re-initiates OAuth; Tap "Contact Support" → opens default mail client
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** "Try Again" → Screen 3

---

### Screen 5 — Home

**Purpose:** Dashboard and ride entry point for authenticated users
**Layout:** Top: greeting + Strava avatar. Middle: "Plan Ride" primary CTA card. Below: Recent rides list (last 3). Bottom: Tab bar (Home, Segments, History, Settings)
**Components:**
- Greeting: "Good morning, Carlos."
- Segment summary chip: "12 starred segments · Last synced 2h ago"
- "Plan Ride" card: prominent, full-width, cycling icon
- Recent rides: list of last 3 rides with date, segment count, PR count
- Tab bar: Home, Segments, History, Settings
**User actions:** Tap "Plan Ride" → Screen 6 (Route Setup); Tap recent ride → Screen 13 (Ride History Detail); Tap Segments tab → Screen 7; Tap History tab → Screen 12; Tap Settings tab → Screen 14
**Empty state (new user):** Replace recent rides with "Your rides will appear here after your first Sherpaa session."
**Navigation:** Tab bar; "Plan Ride" → Route Setup

---

### Screen 6 — Route Setup

**Purpose:** Allow user to identify which starred segments are on today's route and trigger pre-generation
**Layout:** 50/50 vertical split. Top half: dark map (Leaflet rendered in a WebView with CartoDB Dark Matter tiles — no Google Maps SDK, no API key). Bottom half: panel that shows either (a) the ride picker list, or (b) the on-route segment list + goal chips + generate CTA after a ride is confirmed.
**Components:**
- Map (top half): renders the previewed/confirmed ride polyline in gold; when confirmed, also renders gold circle markers at each matched segment start. `fitBounds` pads the top by `insets.top + 56px` so the route doesn't hide under the pill overlay
- Pills overlay (top of map, absolute): `✦ Strava Route` (active), `Import GPX` (shows "Coming soon" alert — stubbed for v1.1), `Skip` (proceeds with all starred segments, no route filter)
- Panel state A — Ride picker (before confirmation): "Select a ride" header; scrollable list of the 10 most recent Strava activities with a GPS polyline. Tap a row to preview (row highlights with gold left-border, map renders polyline). "Use this ride" primary CTA — disabled until a row is selected, enabled button text shows truncated ride name
- Panel state B — Segments + goal (after confirmation): "N segments on route" header + "← Change ride" secondary action; ordered segment list with distance-along-route, best time, and no-history dimming; Goal Mode chips (PR Attempt | Training | Recovery); "Generate Coaching Cues" primary CTA
**User actions:** Tap a ride row to preview; tap "Use this ride" to confirm; tap "Change ride" to reset; tap Goal Mode chips; tap "Generate Coaching Cues" → triggers cue generation; tap Skip pill to bypass route matching entirely
**Empty states:**
- No recent Strava activities with polylines: "No recent rides with routes found."
- No starred segments matched to the route: "No starred segments matched this route." (Generate button still enabled — cues generate for all starred segments)
**Navigation:** No explicit back button (removed — user navigates via tab bar or system back); "Generate Coaching Cues" → Screen 7 (Cue Generation)

---

### Screen 7 — Cue Generation Progress

**Purpose:** Non-blocking progress indicator while LLM generates cue variants
**Layout:** Centered progress state with segment list
**Components:**
- Headline: "Coaching your ride…"
- Progress list: each segment shown with status (pending / generating / done / failed)
- Progress bar (overall %)
- "Skip to Ride" link (if user wants to start with partial cues or cached cues)
- Estimated time: "~30 seconds on WiFi"
**User actions:** Tap "Skip to Ride" → proceeds to Screen 8 with available cues; waits for auto-advance when all complete
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** Auto-advances to Screen 8 (Pre-Ride Brief)

---

### Screen 8 — Pre-Ride Brief (Ready to Ride)

**Purpose:** Final pre-ride confirmation; delivers spoken brief; surfaces Start Ride CTA
**Layout:** Summary card with segment list and goal; large "Start Ride" CTA; speaker icon indicating spoken brief is playing
**Components:**
- Segment list: ordered list of on-route segments with PRs and mode targets
- Goal badge: "Goal: Training Effort"
- Audio waveform animation while spoken brief plays
- "Start Ride" primary button (active after brief completes, or skippable)
- "Edit Goals" secondary button
- Battery and GPS status bar at top
**User actions:** Tap "Start Ride" → Screen 9 (In-Ride); Tap "Edit Goals" → returns to Screen 6 with preserved selections; tap to skip spoken brief
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** "Start Ride" → Screen 9; "Edit Goals" → Screen 6

---

### Screen 9 — In-Ride (Active)

**Purpose:** Minimal ride screen visible if user looks at phone; primary UX is audio
**Layout:** Dark background; minimal data overlay; segment proximity indicator
**Components:**
- Current speed (large, center)
- HR (if connected via Bluetooth HRM; shows "--" if not)
- Distance to next segment (pill indicator at top: "Hawk Hill · 1.2 km")
- Elapsed ride time
- "End Ride" button (bottom, red, requires 2-second hold to prevent accidental activation)
- GPS status icon
- Audio active indicator (subtle waveform when cue playing)
**User actions:** Hold "End Ride" for 2 seconds → triggers Flow 4 (Post-Ride Debrief); tap segment pill → expands to show segment PR and target
**Empty state:** No next segment → pill shows "No more segments · [distance] km ridden"
**Navigation:** Back button hidden during ride; only "End Ride" exits

---

### Screen 10 — Segment Active (In-Ride overlay)

**Purpose:** Contextual overlay that appears when a segment is active (GPS has crossed segment start)
**Layout:** Slides up from bottom of Screen 9; semi-transparent dark card
**Components:**
- Segment name (large)
- Elapsed time on segment (counting up)
- Gap to PR pace (live, updates every 5 seconds: "+3s" in red or "-2s" in green)
- Current speed / power (if available)
- Progress bar (% of segment distance covered)
**User actions:** Tap to dismiss overlay (returns to Screen 9 without interrupting detection)
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** Auto-dismisses at segment end; slides back into Screen 9

---

### Screen 11 — Segment Result (In-Ride interstitial)

**Purpose:** Display segment result immediately after completion; brief on-screen confirmation of spoken result cue
**Layout:** Full-screen flash card, 4-second auto-dismiss
**Components:**
- Segment name
- Final time (large)
- Gap to PR: "NEW PR · -4s" (gold) or "−6s off PR" (neutral)
- PR crown icon if new PR
- Dismiss automatically after 4 seconds or on tap
**User actions:** Tap to dismiss immediately → returns to Screen 9
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** Auto-returns to Screen 9

---

### Screen 12 — Ride History List

**Purpose:** Chronological list of all rides tracked by Sherpaa
**Layout:** Scrollable list, grouped by week
**Components:**
- Ride cards: date, route thumbnail, distance, segment count, PR count
- Filter chips: "All Rides" | "PRs" | "Training"
- Search bar
**User actions:** Tap ride card → Screen 13 (Ride History Detail); pull to refresh
**Empty state:** "No rides yet. Plan your first ride to get started."
**Navigation:** Tab bar; tap card → Screen 13

---

### Screen 13 — Ride History Detail

**Purpose:** Full detail of a completed ride, including segment-by-segment breakdown and cue playback
**Layout:** Scrollable: map at top, stats summary, segment accordion list
**Components:**
- Map: ride track, segment highlights
- Stats: distance, duration, elevation, PR count
- Segment list: each segment as expandable card (time, gap to PR, splits comparison, cue text review)
- "Listen to Debrief Again" button (replays post-ride debrief audio)
- Share button (native share sheet)
**User actions:** Tap segment card → expand/collapse splits detail; tap "Listen to Debrief Again" → replays debrief TTS; tap Share → iOS share sheet
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** Back → Screen 12

---

### Screen 14 — Settings

**Purpose:** Account management, coaching preferences, and subscription control
**Layout:** Grouped list (UITableView-style)
**Components — Sections:**
- **Account:** Strava connection status, Disconnect Strava button, athlete name/photo
- **Coaching Preferences:** Default goal mode, HR zones (manual input or Apple Health import), Power zones (manual FTP input), Cue verbosity (Brief | Standard | Detailed), TTS voice selector (system voices), TTS rate slider
- **Subscription:** Current tier badge, "Manage Subscription" (opens iOS subscription management), "Restore Purchases"
- **Data:** "Refresh Starred Segments" (manual pull), "Clear Cue Cache", Storage usage display
- **About:** Version, Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, Support
**User actions:** Standard settings interactions; toggle switches; text inputs for HR/power zones; tap "Disconnect Strava" → confirmation alert → revokes token, clears data, returns to Welcome screen
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** Tab bar; "Disconnect Strava" → Screen 1

---

### Screen 15 — Segments Explorer

**Purpose:** Browse and inspect all starred segments with historical data
**Layout:** Map + list split view (iPad-style collapsible on iPhone)
**Components:**
- Map: all starred segments as polylines with segment name labels
- List: segment cards sorted by "recently ridden" (default), with PR, last effort date, effort count
- Sort/filter: by PR date, by distance, by region
- Segment card: name, length, elevation, PR time, last 5 effort times (sparkline), coaching status (cues fresh / stale / never generated)
**User actions:** Tap segment on map or list → expands to full segment detail (PR history, splits chart, tendency tags); tap "Generate Cues Now" → triggers single-segment cue generation
**Empty state:** "No starred segments. Open Strava and star the segments you want coaching on."
**Navigation:** Tab bar; segment detail is a push navigation (not modal)

---

### Screen 16 — Paywall / Subscription Upgrade

**Purpose:** Convert Free users to Pro or Elite
**Layout:** Modal, full-screen; dismissible with X button top-right (honors App Store guidelines)
**Components:**
- Headline: "Unlock Your Full Segment Coach"
- Feature comparison table: 3 columns (Free, Pro, Elite) with row-by-row feature checklist
- Plan selector: Pro Annual ($59/yr, "Save 30%"), Pro Monthly ($6.99/mo), Elite Annual ($99/yr, "Save 37%"), Elite Monthly ($12.99/mo)
- Default selected: Pro Annual
- "Start 7-Day Free Trial" CTA (updates to "Subscribe" if trial already used)
- "Restore Purchases" text link
- Legal footer: trial terms, auto-renewal disclosure, cancellation instructions (required by App Store)
**User actions:** Tap plan → select; tap CTA → StoreKit payment sheet; tap X → dismiss (returns to prior screen)
**Empty state:** N/A
**Navigation:** Modal dismiss → returns to prior context

---

## Functional Requirements

**Authentication & Integration**
1. The system shall authenticate users via Strava OAuth 2.0 using `ASWebAuthenticationSession`, requesting `read` and `activity:read_all` scopes, and store access and refresh tokens exclusively in iOS Keychain.
2. The system shall automatically refresh the Strava OAuth access token before expiry (Strava tokens expire after 6 hours) using the stored refresh token, without requiring user re-authentication.
3. Users can manually trigger a re-sync of their starred segments from the Segments Explorer screen or Settings at any time.

**Segment & Data Management**
4. The system shall fetch all starred segments and the last 90 days of effort history per segment from the Strava API at ride planning time (pre-ride), caching all segment coordinate data locally in the device's persistent store.
5. The system shall regenerate coaching cues for a segment when (a) a new PR is set on that segment, or (b) more than 30 days have elapsed since last cue generation; otherwise, cached cue text shall be reused.
6. The system shall store all coaching cue text as strings in the local database and convert to speech on-device using `AVSpeechSynthesizer`; no audio files shall be cached.

**GPS & Geofencing**
7. The system shall poll device GPS at 1Hz during an active ride using CoreLocation's continuous location updates in background mode.
8. The system shall calculate the distance between the rider's current GPS coordinate and each on-route segment start using the Haversine formula; the 500m approach cue shall fire when this distance falls below 500 meters for the first time per segment per ride.
9. The system shall apply a 25-meter tolerance buffer to segment start and end coordinate detection to account for GPS drift and road-level positioning variance.
10. The system shall not fire any LLM calls, Strava API calls, or other network requests during an active ride; all coaching cue delivery shall rely exclusively on locally cached data.

**Coaching Cue Logic**
11. The system shall generate exactly 3 cue variants per segment at ride-planning time — Aggressive, Moderate, and Recovery — using the LLM provider (Claude Haiku or GPT-4o mini); variant selection shall occur at the 500m geofence trigger based on live HR and/or power data, with no network call at selection time.
12. The system shall play mid-segment split cues only when the rider's inferred pace deviates from the cached PR split pace by more than ±5 seconds at the 25%, 50%, and 75% distance checkpoints; no cue fires if pace is on target.
13. Users can set their default goal mode (PR Attempt, Training Effort, or Recovery Ride) in Settings; goal mode may be overridden per ride on the Pre-Ride Brief screen.
14. The system shall deliver between-segment check-in cues only if the rider's heart rate exceeds 85% of their configured maximum HR for more than 5 continuous minutes between segments; silence shall be the default between-segment behavior.

**Audio & Background**
15. The system shall maintain an active `AVAudioSession` with `.playback` category during rides, pausing any concurrently playing audio (music, podcasts) for the duration of each coaching cue before restoring the previous audio session.
16. The system shall queue coaching cues that cannot be delivered immediately (e.g., audio session interrupted by a phone call) and deliver them upon session restoration, skipping any cue that is more than 45 seconds stale.
17. The system shall operate correctly in iOS background mode using the `location` and `audio` background capability entitlements, surviving routine iOS memory pressure events without loss of GPS tracking or cue delivery.

**Subscription & Entitlements**
18. The system shall enforce Free tier limits (maximum 3 starred segments coached per ride, pace-only generic cues, no spoken post-ride debrief) and unlock Pro and Elite features exclusively upon confirmed StoreKit transaction receipt validation.
19. Users can restore previous purchases from the paywall screen or Settings without re-entering payment information, using StoreKit's restore purchases API.
20. The system shall not display the subscription paywall more than once per 48-hour period to a user who has dismissed it without purchasing.

**Post-Ride & Sync**
21. The system shall sync the completed ride activity to Strava via the Strava API after ride end, retrying with exponential backoff (maximum 3 retries) if the initial sync request fails; local effort data shall be preserved until sync succeeds.
22. The system shall update the PacingModel for each completed segment after every ride by recording actual pace at each 25% checkpoint relative to the PR pace, and computing rolling tendency tags (fade, strong-start, consistent) over the last 5 efforts.

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

**Performance**
- NFR1: Geofence trigger to first audio byte output: < 300ms (from trigger detection to `AVSpeechSynthesizer` `.speak()` call)
- NFR2: Pre-ride cue generation (all segments, LLM calls): < 45 seconds on a standard LTE connection for up to 10 segments
- NFR3: App cold start to Home screen: < 2 seconds on iPhone 12 or newer
- NFR4: GPS polling must not cause battery drain exceeding 8% per hour during active ride (benchmark: iPhone 14, background location, 2-hour ride)

**Reliability**
- NFR5: In-ride cue delivery success rate: > 99.5% for pre-cached cues (network-independent path)
- NFR6: Strava sync retry queue shall persist cue data across app restarts until sync succeeds
- NFR7: App must not crash on devices with < 3GB available storage; graceful degradation when storage is full (skip cue cache update, alert user)

**Security**
- NFR8: OAuth tokens stored exclusively in iOS Keychain with `kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlocked` attribute
- NFR9: No Strava user data shall be transmitted to third parties or used to train ML models, in compliance with Strava API Agreement (November 2024); all LLM inputs shall consist of anonymized effort statistics only, not raw Strava activity IDs or athlete identifiers
- NFR10: All network communication shall use TLS 1.3 minimum; certificate pinning for Sherpaa backend endpoints

**Compatibility**
- NFR11: iOS 16.0 minimum deployment target; tested on iPhone 12, 14, 15, and 16 series
- NFR12: App must function correctly with Shokz OpenRun Pro, Apple AirPods (all generations), and generic Bluetooth A2DP headphones; no proprietary audio SDK required

**Privacy**
- NFR13: Location data shall not be transmitted to Sherpaa backend during rides; all GPS processing is on-device
- NFR14: App shall request location permission "While Using" at onboarding; upgrade to "Always" permission shall be requested at first ride start with explicit contextual explanation ("We need background location to detect segments while your phone is in your pocket")
- NFR15: App shall comply with Apple App Store privacy nutrition label requirements; data types collected: Location (used for core functionality, not linked to identity), Usage Data (analytics, linked to identity via Strava ID)

---

## Data Model

### Rider
```
Rider {
  id: UUID (primary key, local)
  stravaAthleteId: Int64 (from Strava API)
  displayName: String
  profileImageURL: String?
  maxHeartRate: Int? (user-configured, used for HR zone calculations)
  ftp: Int? (Functional Threshold Power, user-configured, watts)
  defaultGoalMode: GoalMode (enum: prAttempt | training | recovery)
  subscriptionTier: SubscriptionTier (enum: free | pro | elite)
  subscriptionExpiryDate: Date?
  stravaScopeGranted: [String] (e.g. ["read", "activity:read_all"])
  lastSegmentSyncDate: Date?
  createdAt: Date
  updatedAt: Date
}
```

### Segment
```
Segment {
  id: UUID (primary key, local)
  stravaSegmentId: Int64 (from Strava API)
  name: String
  distance: Float (meters)
  elevationGain: Float (meters)
  averageGrade: Float (percent)
  startLatLng: [Double] (latitude, longitude)
  endLatLng: [Double] (latitude, longitude)
  polylineEncoded: String (Google-encoded polyline of segment path)
  prTime: Int? (seconds; athlete's all-time PR on this segment)
  prDate: Date?
  prSplitPaces: [SplitPace] (array of {checkpointPercent: Int, elapsedSeconds: Int} for 25/50/75/100%)
  recentBestTime: Int? (seconds; best in last 90 days)
  lastBestTime: Int? (seconds; most recent effort)
  lastCueGenerationDate: Date?
  coordinatesCachedAt: Date
  isStarred: Bool
  riderId: UUID (foreign key → Rider)
}
```

### SegmentEffort
```
SegmentEffort {
  id: UUID (primary key, local)
  stravaEffortId: Int64? (null until synced to Strava)
  segmentId: UUID (foreign key → Segment)
  riderId: UUID (foreign key → Rider)
  rideId: UUID (foreign key → Ride)
  elapsedTime: Int (seconds)
  startDate: Date
  averageWatts: Float? (nil if no power meter)
  averageHeartRate: Float? (nil if no HR monitor)
  splitActuals: [SplitActual] (array of {checkpointPercent: Int, elapsedSeconds: Int})
  gapToPR: Int (seconds; negative = faster than PR)
  isNewPR: Bool
  goalModeAtTime: GoalMode
  cueVariantUsed: CueVariant (enum: aggressive | moderate | recovery)
  syncedToStrava: Bool
  createdAt: Date
}
```

### Ride
```
Ride {
  id: UUID (primary key, local)
  stravaActivityId: Int64? (null until synced)
  riderId: UUID (foreign key → Rider)
  startDate: Date
  endDate: Date?
  totalDistance: Float (meters)
  totalElevationGain: Float (meters)
  goalMode: GoalMode
  segmentEffortIds: [UUID] (ordered list of SegmentEfforts during this ride)
  gpxTrackEncoded: String? (compressed GPS track; stored locally; not uploaded to Sherpaa backend)
  debriefText: String? (generated post-ride debrief text)
  syncedToStrava: Bool
  createdAt: Date
}
```

### Cue
```
Cue {
  id: UUID (primary key, local)
  segmentId: UUID (foreign key → Segment)
  riderId: UUID (foreign key → Rider)
  variant: CueVariant (enum: aggressive | moderate | recovery)
  triggerType: CueTriggerType (enum: approach500m | segmentStart | split25 | split50 | split75 | segmentEnd | checkIn | rideStart | preRideBrief | postRideDebrief)
  text: String (LLM-generated or template-generated cue text)
  generatedAt: Date
  generationSource: String (enum: llm | template | fallback)
  llmModel: String? (e.g. "claude-haiku-3-5", "gpt-4o-mini")
  inputTokenCount: Int?
  outputTokenCount: Int?
  isStale: Bool (true if new PR set since generation)
}
```

### PacingModel
```
PacingModel {
  id: UUID (primary key, local)
  segmentId: UUID (foreign key → Segment)
  riderId: UUID (foreign key → Rider)
  effortCount: Int (number of efforts used to build this model)
  tendencyTags: [TendencyTag] (enum values: fade | strongStart | consistent | earlyBlow | strongFinish)
  avgCheckpoint25Deviation: Float (seconds; positive = behind PR pace)
  avgCheckpoint50Deviation: Float
  avgCheckpoint75Deviation: Float
  avgCheckpoint100Deviation: Float
  bestTimeOfDayHour: Int? (hour 0–23 when best times are set; nil if insufficient data)
  lastUpdated: Date
  minimumEffortsForTendency: Int (default: 3; model tags not surfaced until this threshold met)
}
```

---

## API Surface

### Strava OAuth Flow

**Step 1 — Authorization Request**
```
GET https://www.strava.com/oauth/authorize
  ?client_id={STRAVA_CLIENT_ID}
  &redirect_uri={APP_SCHEME}://oauth/callback
  &response_type=code
  &approval_prompt=auto
  &scope=read,activity:read_all
```
- Opened via `ASWebAuthenticationSession`
- `redirect_uri` must use custom URL scheme registered in Info.plist (e.g. `trailvoice://oauth/callback`)

**Step 2 — Token Exchange**
```
POST https://www.strava.com/oauth/token
Body: {
  client_id: String,
  client_secret: String,  // stored in backend, never in app binary
  code: String,           // from OAuth callback
  grant_type: "authorization_code"
}
Response: {
  token_type: "Bearer",
  expires_at: Int (Unix timestamp),
  expires_in: Int (seconds),
  refresh_token: String,
  access_token: String,
  athlete: { id, firstname, lastname, profile }
}
```
**Security note:** `client_secret` must be handled server-side via Sherpaa backend proxy. The iOS app must never hold the client_secret.

**Step 3 — Token Refresh**
```
POST https://www.strava.com/oauth/token
Body: {
  client_id: String,
  client_secret: String,
  refresh_token: String,
  grant_type: "refresh_token"
}
```

### Strava API Calls (Pre-Ride Only)

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Rate limit impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/api/v3/athlete` | GET | Fetch athlete profile | 1 call |
| `/api/v3/segments/starred` | GET | Fetch all starred segments | 1–3 calls (paginated) |
| `/api/v3/segments/{id}` | GET | Fetch segment detail + coordinates | 1 call per segment |
| `/api/v3/athletes/{id}/stats` | GET | Fetch athlete stats | 1 call |
| `/api/v3/segment_efforts?segment_id={id}&per_page=90` | GET | Last 90 days efforts per segment | 1 call per segment |
| `/api/v3/uploads` | POST | Post-ride activity sync | 1 call post-ride |

**Rate limits:** 200 requests per 15 minutes; 2,000 per day. Pre-ride fetch for 20 segments = ~42 calls. Well within limits.

### Sherpaa Internal API

All internal endpoints are authenticated via JWT derived from the Strava OAuth token.

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `POST /api/v1/auth/token` | POST | Exchange Strava OAuth code server-side; return JWT |
| `POST /api/v1/cues/generate` | POST | Trigger LLM cue generation for a segment batch |
| `GET /api/v1/cues/{segmentId}` | GET | Retrieve cached cues for a segment |
| `POST /api/v1/rides` | POST | Submit completed ride metadata for backend record |
| `POST /api/v1/efforts` | POST | Submit segment efforts for pacing model update |
| `GET /api/v1/riders/{id}/pacing-model` | GET | Retrieve pacing model for a rider+segment pair |
| `POST /api/v1/subscriptions/validate` | POST | Validate StoreKit receipt server-side |

**Note:** All GPS data is processed on-device. The Sherpaa backend receives: anonymized effort statistics (elapsed times, checkpoint deviations), cue generation requests (segment stats + tendency data, no athlete identifiers beyond Sherpaa internal rider UUID), and StoreKit receipts. No raw Strava activity data or GPS coordinates are sent to the Sherpaa backend.

---

## Success Metrics

### Primary KPI
**Weekly Active Riders (WAR):** Users who complete at least one coached ride in a 7-day window. Target: 500 WAR by end of Month 2 (beta); 5,000 WAR by Month 6.

### Engagement Metrics
| Metric | 30-day target | 60-day target | 90-day target |
|---|---|---|---|
| Beta signups (waitlist + TestFlight) | 200 | 400 | 600 |
| Rides with at least 1 coached segment | 300 | 1,000 | 2,500 |
| Cue delivery success rate (% of expected cues delivered) | > 98% | > 99% | > 99.5% |
| Avg segments coached per ride | 2.5 | 3.2 | 3.8 |
| D7 retention (riders with 2+ rides in first week) | 40% | 45% | 50% |
| D30 retention | 25% | 30% | 35% |

### Conversion Metrics
| Metric | 30-day | 60-day | 90-day |
|---|---|---|---|
| Free → Pro conversion rate | 12% | 18% | 22% |
| Free → Elite conversion rate | 3% | 5% | 7% |
| Pro → Elite upgrade rate | 8% | 12% | 15% |
| Trial-to-paid conversion | 45% | 50% | 55% |

### Quality Metrics
- Cue relevance rating (in-app thumbs up/down after each ride): > 75% positive by Day 60
- Post-ride debrief completions (listened to full debrief): > 60% of rides
- Crash-free sessions: > 99.8%
- App Store rating: > 4.5 stars by Day 90

### North Star
**Coached segment efforts per month** — the raw count of times a rider entered a segment with Sherpaa active and received cues. This metric captures both rider count and ride frequency, and is the truest proxy for product value delivery.

---

## Launch Criteria

### Technical — Must Pass Before Any User Touches the App

- [ ] OAuth flow completes end-to-end on physical device (not simulator) for 3 test accounts
- [ ] Cue generation succeeds for a 10-segment route in < 45 seconds on LTE
- [ ] Geofence triggers correctly on Hawk Hill, Paradise Loop, and Mt. Tam test routes (GPS field test)
- [ ] All 3 cue variants generated and correct variant selected based on HR input (unit test + field test)
- [ ] In-ride flow completes with zero network calls during ride (Charles Proxy verification)
- [ ] App survives 2-hour background ride on iPhone 14 without crash or GPS loss
- [ ] Battery drain during 2-hour ride: < 16% (8%/hr threshold)
- [ ] Post-ride Strava sync succeeds within 60 seconds of ride end on LTE
- [ ] App handles Strava API rate limit gracefully (returns cached data; no crash)
- [ ] OAuth token refresh works silently when token is expired
- [ ] Offline ride completes with cached cues (airplane mode test)
- [ ] Free tier enforcement: 4th segment produces paywall, not crash
- [ ] StoreKit sandbox purchase and restore work correctly on TestFlight
- [ ] All coaching cue text passes manual review: no incorrect segment names, no phantom PRs

### Legal / Compliance — Must Pass Before Public Beta

- [ ] Strava API Agreement compliance audit: confirm no athlete data sent to LLM with identifiable attributes
- [ ] Privacy Policy published and linked in app
- [ ] App Store privacy nutrition label accurately reflects data collection
- [ ] 7-day free trial terms disclosed in paywall and App Store listing per App Store Review Guidelines 3.1.2
- [ ] `ASWebAuthenticationSession` used (not deprecated SFSafariViewController or in-app WebView)

### Beta Readiness — Must Pass Before Inviting 200 Beta Riders

- [ ] TestFlight build uploaded; App Review passed (first external build review: allow 24–48 hours)
- [ ] 50 Bay Area segments pre-loaded and coordinate-tested
- [ ] Feedback mechanism in app (in-ride thumbs up/down; post-ride NPS) functional
- [ ] Crash reporting (e.g., Sentry or Crashlytics) integrated and receiving events
- [ ] Onboarding email sequence configured for beta invitees
- [ ] Club partnership confirmed: Alto Velo and MCBC contacts have TestFlight access

---

## Revenue Model

### Pricing Tiers

| Tier | Monthly | Annual | Annual per month |
|---|---|---|---|
| Free | $0 | $0 | — |
| Pro | $6.99 | $59.00 | $4.92 |
| Elite | $12.99 | $99.00 | $8.25 |

### Unit Economics

| Item | Cost per ride |
|---|---|
| GPS processing | $0 (on-device) |
| Strava API calls | $0 (free tier) |
| TTS (AVSpeechSynthesizer) | $0 (on-device) |
| LLM cue generation (Claude Haiku / GPT-4o mini) | ~$0.004 |
| Backend infrastructure (compute + storage per ride) | ~$0.002 |
| **Total COGS per ride** | **~$0.006** |

At 12 rides/month (Pro tier): COGS = $0.072/month. Revenue = $6.99/month. **Gross margin = ~99%.**
At 15 rides/month (Elite tier): COGS = $0.09/month. Revenue = $12.99/month. **Gross margin = ~99.3%.**

*Apple takes 30% of IAP revenue in Year 1 (reduces to 15% after 1 year per Apple Small Business Program if revenue < $1M). Effective gross margin after Apple cut: ~69% (Year 1), ~84% (Year 2+).*

### Year 1–3 Revenue Projection

**Assumptions:**
- Beta: 200 riders (Month 1–3); Public launch: Month 4
- Growth: organic + influencer, 20% MoM after launch
- Tier mix: 65% Free, 28% Pro, 7% Elite (steady state)
- Annual plan take rate: 40% of paid subscribers

| Period | Total Riders | Paid Riders | MRR | ARR |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Month 3 (beta close) | 200 | 40 | $350 | — |
| Month 6 (post-launch) | 800 | 240 | $2,200 | — |
| Month 12 | 3,500 | 1,050 | $9,100 | $109,000 |
| Year 2 (Month 24) | 12,000 | 3,600 | $31,000 | $372,000 |
| Year 3 (Month 36) | 32,000 | 9,600 | $83,000 | $996,000 |

*Year 3 approaches $1M ARR, triggering renegotiation of Apple Small Business Program eligibility. At this scale, infrastructure costs remain < 1% of revenue.*

**Breakeven:** Estimated Month 8–10, assuming 2-person founding team with no external salaries (founder-funded to beta).

---

## Risks

### Risk 1 — Strava API Restriction (CRITICAL)
**Description:** Strava's November 2024 API Agreement update prohibits third parties from using Strava data "in artificial intelligence models or similar applications." Sherpaa uses Strava segment history as input to LLM cue generation. This is a direct collision.

**Probability:** High (the restriction is explicit in current API terms)

**Impact:** If enforced, Strava could revoke API access, removing the core data source.

**Mitigations:**
1. **Legal interpretation path:** Strava's own clarification stated that "coaching platforms focused on providing feedback to users" are in the <0.1% of affected apps they expected — and are explicitly still allowed. Sherpaa sends *anonymized effort statistics* (times, pace differentials), not raw activity data or athlete IDs, to the LLM. The LLM receives no data that could identify a Strava user. This is a strong legal argument.
2. **Structural mitigation:** Design the LLM prompt to contain zero Strava-originated identifiers. Input = `{segmentDistance: 2.1km, riderPRSeconds: 252, checkpoint50Deviation: +8s, tendencyTag: "fade"}`. No Strava segment IDs, no athlete IDs.
3. **Escalation path:** Apply for Strava's Partner API program as product scales. Strava has a history of retrospectively partnering with apps that demonstrate user demand.
4. **Worst-case contingency:** If Strava revokes access, pivot to user-uploaded GPX + manual segment definition as a data source. Product continues with degraded data quality.

**Owner:** CEO/Founder — must monitor Strava developer communications weekly.

### Risk 2 — iOS Background Location Reliability
**Description:** iOS aggressively terminates background processes to preserve battery. If the app is suspended mid-ride, geofence triggers may be missed, causing cues to not fire.

**Probability:** Medium (iOS 16+ improved background location reliability for declared use cases)

**Impact:** Missed cues during rides = core product failure. High user dissatisfaction.

**Mitigations:**
1. Register for `CLLocationManager` significant-location-change monitoring as a fallback to continuous location updates
2. Use `CLCircularRegion` iOS-native geofencing (processed by the OS even when app is suspended) as a secondary trigger mechanism alongside the 1Hz polling approach
3. Implement a "cue missed" detection: if a segment start coordinate was passed (inferred from track reconstruction post-ride) but no cue was delivered, log for quality review
4. TestFlight beta must include a 2-hour background ride test on all target device models

### Risk 3 — Audio Interruption During Segments
**Description:** Phone calls, Siri activations, CarPlay handoffs, or system notifications can interrupt `AVAudioSession`, causing mid-segment cues to be silenced or delayed.

**Probability:** Medium (phone calls during rides are common; other interruptions less so)

**Impact:** Missed segment start or split cues breaks the flow; riders notice and lose trust quickly.

**Mitigations:**
1. Implement `AVAudioSession` interruption handler: queue cues that are interrupted; deliver within 10 seconds of resumption if still relevant (within segment distance)
2. Apply staleness logic: if an approach cue would fire > 45 seconds after trigger (e.g., phone call lasted 2 minutes and segment has already started), skip the approach cue and play the start cue instead
3. Do not fight CarPlay; document CarPlay compatibility as a v1.1 feature

### Risk 4 — Cue Quality and Personalization Accuracy
**Description:** The value proposition rests on cue language feeling genuinely personal. If cues sound generic or contain incorrect data (wrong PR, wrong segment name, fabricated tendencies), riders will churn immediately.

**Probability:** Low-Medium (LLMs can hallucinate; tendency tags require minimum 3 efforts to form)

**Impact:** High — first-ride cue quality determines whether the rider tells their club about the app or uninstalls it.

**Mitigations:**
1. All LLM-generated cue text is persisted in the Cue table; review tooling built for internal QA before beta
2. Tendency tags gated behind `minimumEffortsForTendency` (default 3); first-ride cues use generic-but-accurate template language, not fabricated patterns
3. LLM prompt must include explicit instructions: "Use only the statistics provided. Do not infer or invent rider behavior. If insufficient data exists, use a generic encouragement cue."
4. Post-ride thumbs up/down rating on cue quality feeds directly into a cue review queue; any segment with > 20% thumbs down triggers human review + re-generation

---

## PM Assumptions

1. **Strava API compliance is achievable** through careful LLM input design (anonymized stats only, no raw Strava data passed to LLM). This assumption has not been validated with Strava's developer relations team. Must be validated before beta launch.

2. **LLM cue generation completes within 45 seconds for 10 segments** on LTE. This is based on Haiku/GPT-4o mini latency benchmarks at < 1,000 tokens per request. Has not been tested under real network conditions. Must be load-tested before launch.

3. **The Bay Area pilot market (Alto Velo, MCBC) is representative of the broader road cycling segment hunter persona.** If beta users are disproportionately competitive racers (unlike Diana or Priya), the tone of default cues may need adjustment before broader launch.

4. **iOS AVSpeechSynthesizer voice quality is acceptable to riders.** This is a cost-driven architectural decision. If beta feedback consistently rates voice quality as off-putting, ElevenLabs or Amazon Polly voices (cached as audio at generation time) become the v1.1 upgrade path.

5. **Strava starred segments are a reliable proxy for "segments the rider cares about."** In practice, some riders have 50+ starred segments from years of casual starring. The app will process all of them, but route-intersection filtering at pre-ride time will manage this. No artificial cap on starred segments is imposed.

6. **Apple will approve the app under its standard App Store review process.** Background location, always-on audio session, and IAP are all within standard App Store guidelines when implemented correctly. No novel entitlements are required.

7. **200 beta riders from cycling clubs is a sufficient signal.** This is a conveniently organized, high-engagement group. Their feedback may skew toward more serious cyclists. A second cohort from Strava Community Hub waitlist signups will provide diversity.

---

## Open Questions

1. **Strava Partner API:** Has Vein or the founding team made contact with Strava's developer relations team to discuss Sherpaa's intended use case under the November 2024 API terms? A formal conversation could either (a) confirm the current approach is acceptable or (b) surface the need to apply for partner status before beta. This is the highest-priority open question.

2. **Power meter data path:** How does the app receive real-time power data during rides? Strava does not provide live power readings — this must come from a Bluetooth power meter (Favero Assioma, Quarq, etc.) via CoreBluetooth. This is required for Elite tier power coaching cues. The v1 spec assumes HR-only for variant selection; power support requires CoreBluetooth implementation scoped for v1.1 or Elite-tier v1 feature.

3. **LLM provider selection and fallback:** The spec references "Claude Haiku or GPT-4o mini" interchangeably. A binding provider decision needs to be made before backend build begins, as prompt formats, rate limits, pricing tiers, and latency profiles differ. Is there a preference based on existing API relationships, or should this be benchmarked head-to-head against a set of 50 real segment cue generation requests?
